extraction.one_line_summary: PORTE Richie won Tour de Suisse GC after dominating the Stage 9 ITT, holding off KRUIJSWIJK Steven who surged to second overall.
tactical_patterns: KRUIJSWIJK Steven clawed back all but 27 seconds on PORTE Richie through mountain stages after 75-second team time trial loss.
BMC saved PORTE Richie's GC on earlier stage through VAN AVERMAET Greg's domestique work, revealing their emergency response protocols.
MATTHEWS Michael's 4th place in 35km TT signals expanded capacity beyond short prologues, changes sprinter handicapping for long ITTs.

rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: commanded ITT victory to seal GC, BMC's team unity under sponsor uncertainty translated to protective domestique support
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: positive: jumped QUINTANA Nairo to second overall via ITT, confirming pre-Tour test event specialization like 2017 Dauphiné win
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: 2nd in ITT at age 23 plus stage win reveals major engine development, Sunweb's next Dumoulin archetype
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: lost 55 seconds to PORTE Richie and dropped to 3rd overall, exposes ITT vulnerability before cobbles/TT-heavy Tour
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: 86th in ITT after crumbling in final mountain stages, either sandbagging or concerning form collapse before Tour
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: 4th in 35km ITT vastly exceeds prologue-only TT capacity, expands threat profile beyond traditional sprinter handicapping
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: super domestique work saved PORTE Richie on mountain stage, demonstrates ITT specialists' climbing utility under pressure
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: rode front in leader's jersey earlier, demonstrating BMC's depth in combining ITT power with stage racing workload
